title: Dense Kenyan Lentil Dumplings
description: A buttery, dense, anti-inflammatory Kenyan dish featuring filo pastry and leaf-wrap steam, perfect for a romantic date night.
instructions: Rinse the lentils under cold water until the water runs clear.
In a saucepan, combine lentils and water, bring to a boil, then lower heat and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until lentils are tender and liquid is mostly absorbed.
While the lentils cook, heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat and sauté the chopped onion until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in cumin, coriander, turmeric, and a pinch of salt, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Remove the skillet from heat and mix the sautéed onion mixture into the cooked lentils, then add lemon juice and adjust seasoning.
Fold in a tablespoon of melted butter to enrich the mixture, then let the lentil filling cool slightly.
Lay a sheet of filo pastry on a clean surface, brush lightly with melted butter, and repeat with 5 more sheets, stacking them.
Spoon the lentil filling onto the center of the stacked filo, then fold the sheets over the filling to create a dense dumpling, sealing the edges with a little water.
Steam the dumplings in a steamer basket for 10 minutes, or until the filo turns golden brown and crisp.
Serve hot, optionally drizzled with extra butter or a squeeze of fresh lemon.
